title: consolidation gap: re-add import-linter as toggle + contracts LOCAL-SLOT (all 3 repos use it)
description: Audit of cardiac/mindscape/synth: ALL THREE enforce import-linter (>=1 forbidden contract, min 'core is an independent kernel'). Scaffold dropped it in the de-opinionate refactor arguing contracts are project-specific + graph.py covers cycles. But graph.py _cycles catches only bidirectional cycles, NOT one-way forbidden imports (core->trainer no-cycle passes graph, fails import-linter). The GATE is universal; only the CONTRACTS vary. Fix: enable_import_linter bool (default true); ships [tool.importlinter] with root_packages=packages + a kernel-independence starter contract (packages[0] forbids importing packages[1:]) in a LOCAL-SLOT, ONLY when >1 package (single-package = no layers). Wire lint-imports into nox/ci/pre-commit under the same guard. Render assertion + a bite-test (inject upward import -> fails).

title: de-opinionate template layout: drop core/viewer layers, single package, arch-fitness layer-agnostic
description: Scaffold was shipping the 3-repo core->trainer->viewer layering as imposed structure. Guardrails must be layout-agnostic. Drop core/ + viewer layer + has_viewer/viewer_name/viewer_imports_trainer toggles. Ship ONE package {{ package_name }} (two internal modules: math_ops<-pipeline, keeps an import edge + astgrep-compliant). Remove import-linter from defaults (directional contracts are project-specific; graph.py covers cycles) -> document as opt-in. graph.py --assert stays as the layer-agnostic arch gate (god/cycle/file-size). Update pyproject/noxfile/ci/pre-commit/conftest/test_e2e/copier.yml/docs. Re-green E2E.

title: Tier 2: ml-typing bundle — jaxtyping + beartype + shape_contracts (domain=ml, advisory)
description:
Parent: sdlc-scaffold-vip. Tier 2 = the ml-domain runtime-typing capability. 1/3 today (cardioseg only; mindscape/synthscape carry NEITHER), so it ships gated on domain==ml and ADVISORY — an opt-in-by-domain bundle, the ONE honest exception to the no-toggles rule (rji), justified because it is meaningless off a tensor codebase.

THE BUNDLE (pull from cardiac-seg):
- deps: jaxtyping + beartype (add to template pyproject deps, inside the {% if enable_ml %} block next to numpy). jaxtyping = Float[Tensor,"B C H W"] shape/dtype annotations (inert at runtime without a checker -> no prod cost); beartype = the runtime checker jaxtyping delegates to; cardioseg wraps them as @shapecheck in core.types, enforced in TESTS.
- ruff: ignore = ["F722"] — REQUIRED, jaxtyping shape strings ("b c h w") look like malformed forward refs to F722; jaxtypings own FAQ prescribes disabling it. Add F722 to the scaffolds ruff ignore, gated so it only appears for domain=ml (a non-ml repo has no jaxtyping strings, keep F722 enforced there). Decide: is the ignore list domain-conditional in the template, or F722 always-ignored (harmless if no jaxtyping)?
- shape_contracts.py — promote from cardiac-seg/devtools/ into template/devtools/ (MOVED here from the old 7g0 scope). --assert gate over tensor-shape BOUNDARIES (23 annotated in cardioseg); jaxtyping-style. domain==ml only. Engine+tests template-owned; portable params = jaxtyping type-name list; LOCAL-SLOT = array-aliases (each repos core.types alias names differ — Volume/Mask/... in cardioseg) via a [tool.shape_contracts] block or an inferred convention. Ship ADVISORY.
- The @shapecheck convention: decide whether the scaffold ships a starter core/types shapecheck helper + a docs note, or just the deps + gate and leaves the convention to the repo.

WIRING: advisory CI + nox step gated {% if enable_ml %}, like the class-shape gate #8. E2E: an ml-combo pos/neg fixture (an unannotated tensor boundary bites shape_contracts --assert); the non-ml combo must NOT ship any of it (assert jaxtyping/beartype/F722/shape_contracts absent).

OPEN (walk w/ owner): (a) advisory-forever vs ratchet-to-blocking for shape_contracts; (b) exact domain-gating seam for the ruff F722 ignore; (c) ship the @shapecheck helper or not; (d) array-alias parameterization (config list vs infer from core.types). Since its 1/3, this stays advisory until a 2nd repo adopts — then revisit promoting to a stronger default.

ROLLOUT: with the Tier-1 tag or its own. copier update cardiac-seg replaces its local shape_contracts.py; mindscape/synthscape get the bundle only if they set domain=ml AND opt in (both are ml domain but dont run typing yet — adoption is forward, not forced).

Adds SPEC gate #11 (shape_contracts, ml-only) + the ml-typing dep note. Origin: cardiac-seg-1ln7 / qqjs; jaxtyping bd zwno.

title: EPIC: converge the 3 repos' guardrail architecture through the scaffold (staged adoption)
description:
Current reality (audited 2026-07-14, pyproject+devtools+deps across all 3): the three repos have DIVERGED on the guardrail rule-set, which violates the north star. cardioseg ratcheted furthest (added ~20 ruff codes: N/SIM/SLF001/PERF/C408-420/S603-607/PTH123/E741-3/PLR0124-1714/ICN001/E402, a pep8-naming block, jaxtyping+beartype runtime typing, and 2 new devtools engines magic_literals+shape_contracts). mindscape ratcheted a DIFFERENT direction (broad families C4/UP/SIM/PLR/T20/RUF; explicitly REJECTED N at 383 hits; has its own devtools/analytics.py). synthscape still sits on the scaffold`s EXACT narrow v0.6.0 list. scaffold v0.6.0 ships the narrow curated list.

GOAL: pull the generic/architecture deltas UP into the scaffold and roll them back out so the 3 reconverge — staged by EVIDENCE (majority of 3), gently, starting simple. Broaden adoption over time (get synth+mind onto each new scaffold rule, not just cardioseg). This epic tracks the convergence; children are the staged tiers.

STAGING (owner-endorsed "start simple, tiers 1+2 first, adopt more forward"):
- Tier 1 (child, start here): additive + majority/universal — magic_literals engine (portable, any repo) + add SIM to the scaffold superset (2/3: cardio+mind). See child.
- Tier 2 (child): ml-domain typing bundle — jaxtyping+beartype+shape_contracts+ruff ignore F722, gated on domain==ml, shipped ADVISORY (1/3 today, so opt-in-by-domain, the one honest exception to no-toggles). See child.
- Tier 3 (child): AMBIGUOUS placement — the rest of cardioseg`s ruff ratchet (N, SLF001, PERF, S603/607, PTH123, C4/UP, the pep8-naming mechanism + ML ignore-names slot). NOT dropped and NOT "permanent local" — parked pending 3-repo consensus on which become house-bar. Filed so the gap is tracked, not lost.

CROSS-CUTTING: as each rule graduates into the scaffold superset, the rollout = tag scaffold -> copier update cardiac-seg (origin, replace local copies) -> mindscape -> synthscape (sequence around synthscape-5vh v0.6.0 update). The measure of done for the epic: the generic gate config is copier-generated + identical across the 3; only LOCAL-SLOT facts differ.

AUDIT BOUNDARY (honest): diffed pyproject + devtools filenames + deps across the 3. NOT yet diffed: CI workflow YAML, the @shapecheck convention internals (core.types), the deferred `ty` type-checker axis (cardioseg bd rp8l), mindscape`s analytics.py engine. Those may surface more gaps -> add children as found.
design: NORTH STAR (owner): the GENERIC ARCHITECTURE is identical across cardiac-seg / mindscape / synthscape — engines (devtools/*.py), gate wiring (nox/CI/pre-commit), the RULE SET (ruff select, the gate inventory), and structure. There is NO permanent project-local ARCHITECTURE. The only per-project thing is genuine FACTS filling LOCAL-SLOTs: scan paths = `packages`, domain thresholds, pep8 ignore-names VOCAB (each repo`s physics/ML symbols), vulture domain FPs. Mechanism identical; only slot CONTENTS differ. EVERYTHING flows through the copier pipe — divergence is drift to be healed, not a steady state.

title: Tier 1: promote magic_literals engine + add SIM to the scaffold ruff superset
description:
Parent: sdlc-scaffold-vip (convergence epic). Tier 1 = additive + majority/universal, the safe start.

SCOPE (shape_contracts + the jaxtyping/beartype bundle MOVED to the Tier 2 child — this bead is now magic_literals + SIM only):

1) magic_literals.py — promote from cardiac-seg/devtools/ into template/devtools/ (scaffold gate class "OURS, advisory", same as data_clumps/state_candidates/lcom). Owns the axis ruff PLR2004 cant: NON-comparison string literals + cross-file FREQUENCY + dict-schema key-sets (deduped vs ruff: ruff owns comparison operands, the detector excludes them). In cardioseg its a BLOCKING count-ratchet (--max-strings 48 --max-key-sets 11); in the scaffold ship ADVISORY first (a fresh gen has 0; each repo sets + ratchets its own floor in a LOCAL-SLOT config block).
   - Engine + unit tests = template-owned (tests/unit/test_devtools.py, equivalence-class). Ship verbatim (literal f-string braces -> NO .jinja).
   - Portable params = superset: token regex, default thresholds.
   - Project facts = LOCAL-SLOT / a [tool.magic_literals] block the engine reads: scan paths=packages, project stopwords, floor numbers.
   - Wiring: advisory CI + nox step like gate #8. E2E pos/neg fixture (a magic literal over floor bites).
   - domain gating: NONE — magic strings are universal, always-on (honors rji: no new enable_* toggle).

2) SIM (flake8-simplify) — add to the scaffolds ruff_select superset. Majority-justified: cardioseg AND mindscape both run it (2/3); synthscape doesnt yet. House-bar graduation, not a one-repo fit. The copier-update rollout brings synthscape onto it.

ROLLOUT: tag scaffold -> copier update cardiac-seg (origin; replace its local magic_literals.py copy) -> mindscape -> synthscape. First real template-OUTPUT change since v0.6.0 -> genuine consumer delta -> DO tag.

OPEN (walk w/ owner): magic_literals config surface ([tool.magic_literals] block vs CLI args vs copier answer); advisory-vs-ratchet default in the template (lean advisory, repo ratchets locally). SIM: any sub-code exclusions or whole-family.

Adds SPEC gate #10 (magic_literals). Origin: cardiac-seg-1ln7 / epic qqjs.
close_reason: Tier 1 landed: magic_literals.py promoted into template/devtools (stdlib logging, cardioseg refs stripped, detector logic verbatim, shipped ADVISORY - opt-in --max-* ratchet); wired advisory into nox/CI/pre-commit; SIM added to ruff_select superset (2/3 majority). Unit tests (pos/neg detector + ratchet bite) + e2e (ships-check, advisory-runs, end-to-end ratchet bite). 66 passed. SPEC gate #10 + README. Rollout: tag v0.7.0 -> copier update the 3 repos.

title: Drop ship_example: template ships zero code; e2e owns its fixture; handle empty cold-start
description:
Owner call (synthscape mq4 review): ship_example is another copier toggle that mostly muddies the mission. The scaffold claims guardrails-not-architecture, but ship_example=true ships a demo package (math_ops + pipeline) = shipping CODE. For the real use case (adoption into an existing repo — all 3 siblings) it is always false = dead weight. For a new project the demo is throwaway you delete in 30s.

The ONE thing it buys: a brand-new EMPTY repo generates red (coverage --fail-under=80 on 0%, graph.py --assert on empty packages). That cold-start crutch must be handled elsewhere before removing:

PLAN:
1. Move the demo to an E2E-ONLY fixture. The scaffold e2e currently relies on the shipped demo to have code to lint/test/graph; instead have the e2e generate a project THEN drop its own tiny module + test, then run gates. Template stops shipping any code.
2. Cold-start for a real new project: either (a) gates tolerate empty (coverage/test-mirror advisory until the first module lands), or (b) a one-line README new-project step: write your first module, then gates go green. Prefer (b) — do not weaken gates for the empty case.
3. Remove the ship_example toggle + package_name computed var + the {% if not ship_example %} _exclude entries. copier.yml shrinks.
4. MIGRATION: synthscape adopted at v0.4.2 with ship_example=false already, so its copier update to the toggle-less tag must resolve cleanly (drop the answer, no re-prompt). Test copier update on the synthscape checkout.

Pairs with sdlc-scaffold-rji (enable_* -> one multiselect). Both trim the copier surface toward: minimal prompts, template ships zero code.

title: copier.yml: collapse the enable_* bools into ONE multiselect (or profile), not 5 yes/no prompts
description:
Owner call (from synthscape mq4 review): the optional gates are 5+ independent bool prompts — enable_import_linter, enable_astgrep, enable_jscpd, enable_class_shape_smells, enable_beads (+ enforce_arch_fitness). Prompt sprawl at copy time + a combinatorial toggle-lattice the e2e must cover. Not really independent yes/no axes worth answering one-by-one.

PROPOSAL: one question instead. (A) multiselect list gates (copier multiselect, choices import_linter/astgrep/jscpd/class_shape/beads) — one prompt, answers file stores a list; or (B) profile enum minimal|standard|full mapping to fixed gate sets. Prefer A (granularity in one prompt) unless owner wants opinionated profiles.

TEMPLATE CHANGES: every if-enable_X in templates + copier.yml _exclude becomes an if-X-in-gates membership test. ci.yml / pyproject / devtools file-exclusion all key off it.

MIGRATION (must not break adopters): synthscape is adopted at v0.4.2 with the enable_* bool answers. A copier update to the new shape needs either (a) a copier _migrations hook mapping old bools -> the list, or (b) keep enable_* as computed when:false values derived from gates for back-compat, so old .copier-answers still resolve. Test copier update from v0.4.2 -> new tag on synthscape: NO re-answering, NO gate loss.

enforce_arch_fitness: decide if it joins the list or just becomes always-on (graph.py structural gate is near-core; likely drop its toggle entirely).

title: gitignore: anchor data/ -> /data/ (unanchored pattern hides a core/data/ package's tests)
description: template/.gitignore.jinja ships `data/` unanchored (meaning 'datasets out of repo'). But a project with a package path like core/data/ (synthscape) has tests at tests/unit/core/data/ — the bare `data/` matches THAT too, silently git-ignoring real test files. Found during synthscape mq4 adoption: tests/unit/core/data/test_synth.py was ignored -> CI graph.py --assert failed (missing mirror test), while local passed (file was on disk, just untracked). Fix: anchor to `/data/` (top-level only). Audit the rest of the gitignore for other unanchored dir patterns (runs/, node_modules/ are fine as those aren't source; data/ is the collision because it's a common package name).

title: Reduce folder-structure assumptions: test-mirror hard-codes tests/unit mirror layout
description:
AUDIT (from synthscape c3u adoption re-audit, owner flag: scaffold shouldn't assume folder structure beyond tests/). The scaffold claims 'guardrails, not architecture — imposes no layering' (README) but the test-mirror gate DOES impose a test-tree architecture: devtools/graph.py unmirrored() hard-defaults test_root='tests/unit' and requires tests/unit/<source-path>/test_<name>.py — i.e. the test tree MUST mirror the source tree. A project with a FLAT tests/unit/ (synthscape) is forced to restructure (~24 file moves) purely to satisfy layout, not correctness.

The imposition is not exposed anywhere adoptable: test_root is a def-arg, not a [tool.structure] slot or copier answer. So a project can't declare 'my tests are flat' or 'at tests/' without editing canonical gate code (which then conflicts on copier update).

DECIDE (one of):
1. Make test-mirror layout configurable — test_root + a mirror|flat mode in [tool.structure] LOCAL-SLOT (flat = 'a test_<name>.py exists ANYWHERE under tests/', mirror = current strict path). Lets flat-layout repos adopt without restructuring.
2. Keep strict-mirror but make it OPT-IN (an answer, default off) — mirror is a real discipline (one home per module) but not universal; don't force it on adoption.
3. Justify strict-mirror as a universal house rule and accept the restructure cost (then update the README 'no architecture' claim — it's not true, test layout IS imposed).

Lesser item: devtools/ is a fixed tool home (referenced in ci/nox/pre-commit/sgconfig). Probably acceptable (scaffold's own files) — note but likely no action. NON-issue: no research/ or src/ assumptions found; testpaths=tests is fine. Owner: keep optional toggles MINIMAL (pydantic stays baked, not a toggle) — so prefer option 2 or a single [tool.structure] key over a new copier answer if possible.

title: consolidation gap: add file_min/_undersized advisory to graph.py + [tool.structure]
description: All 3 repos' graph.py has an _undersized advisory rule (line floor, off when file_min<=0) + file_min=0 in [tool.structure]. Scaffold's graph.py lacks both. Add _undersized(files, file_min) advisory + file_min to _DEFAULTS + [tool.structure] slot (default 0=off). Unit-test _undersized pos/neg.

title: consolidation gap: jscpd default enforced not advisory (2/3 repos block)
description: cardiac + mindscape ENFORCE jscpd (block >1% dup); only synth is advisory. Scaffold ships advisory-default on a wrong 'majority' read. Flip enable_jscpd to BLOCKING when on (drop success_codes/continue-on-error in ci+nox), matching the 2/3 majority; document a repo can loosen. jscpd inject-test already asserts non-zero on dup -> still valid.

title: remote: flip pre-commit delivery repo:local -> repo:<url> rev:vN and prove the hook resolves
description: The generated .pre-commit-config uses repo:local (no-git MVP). Remote path: a consumer references repo:<scaffold-url> rev:v0.1.0 with the ids from .pre-commit-hooks.yaml (ruff-gate/vulture-gate/arch-fitness). Prove pre-commit resolves the remote manifest + runs the hooks. The version drift-guard (test_scaffold_consistency) already protects the manifest pins.

title: remote: prove copier update from the remote URL (drift heal + pin advance)
description: After the remote exists: in a throwaway downstream clone generated from v0.1.0, bump a PORTABLE rule in the scaffold, tag v0.2.0, push; run copier update (fetching by URL) in the downstream; assert _commit advances v0.1.0->v0.2.0, the portable change flows in, a LOCAL-SLOT edit survives, no .rej. Mirrors the existing WSL test_copier_update_heals... but against a real remote, not a local tag.

title: remote: create the scaffold's remote repo + first push + tag v0.1.0 baseline
description: NEEDS USER: pick the remote (GitHub public/private, or other) and authorize the push. Then: git remote add origin <url>; git push; git tag v0.1.0; git push --tags. This is the baseline pin every consumer references. Blocks the rest of sdlc-scaffold-8r7.

title: verification milestone: remote hop (repo:url + copier update from a real remote) unproven
description: GAP4 from hn8 audit: repo:local -> repo:<url> rev:vN delivery + copier update from a REAL remote are asserted, not shown. All green locally/WSL but the network hop is stubbed (.pre-commit-hooks.yaml is the remote-delivery manifest, unused until push). NOT a code fix — needs: (1) push scaffold to a remote, (2) tag vN, (3) a downstream repo runs copier update fetching by URL + a pre-commit repo:<url> hook resolves. Do when the user authorizes a remote push. Until then 'versioned central gates' stays an honest known-untested claim.

title: [external audit] post-refactor debt: stale LEARNINGS, nox≠CI parity, un-single-sourced hooks manifest
description:
Filed by an EXTERNAL agent — a Claude session running in the `interviews` project, read-only audit of sdlc-scaffold @ 8a95ed0. NOT the scaffold's own maintainer agent. Verify claims against the tree before acting; some are "verify", not "confirmed".

Context: audit run right after the big refactor (viewer layer + core/ split + import-linter gate removed → single-package `packages`-list model; tool versions single-sourced; `ship_example` added). Refactor direction is good and the E2E was migrated correctly (ship_example=false adoption path + multi-package both tested). Debt below is the residue.

=== GAP 1 — docs/LEARNINGS.md is stale (highest-value, cheap) ===
25 viewer/import-linter references describing the PRE-refactor world (viewer 1→3 import-linter contracts, import-linter as a green core gate, core/ layer) — none of which ships now. SPEC.md was refreshed; LEARNINGS was not.
Worse: the "Migration path" recipe presets `viewer_imports_trainer=` / `has_viewer=` — flags that no longer exist in copier.yml. So the documented way to adopt the 3 real repos is broken against the current template.
Fix: rewrite LEARNINGS to the post-refactor contract; drop viewer/import-linter/core-layer; fix the migration presets to the current toggle set (packages, ship_example, enforce_arch_fitness, enable_astgrep/jscpd/class_shape, coverage_floor).

=== GAP 2 — local nox ≠ CI (leaks the headline claim) ===
noxfile.py.jinja `lint` is a WEAKER gate than ci.yml.jinja:
- CI runs advisory-full `ruff check . --statistics` + `ruff format --check .` over the whole tree AND vulture conf60 advisory. nox does format-check over LAYERS only, no advisory-full, no conf60. Dev passes nox, eats surprises in CI.
- VERIFY (possible harder bug): nox `lint` calls `graph.py --assert` (needs grimp/networkx from the `devtools` extra) but that session never `uv sync --extra devtools`; CI's tests-job does (ci.yml:74). If `uv run` doesn't auto-pull the optional extra, arch-fitness ERRORS under `nox lint` locally while passing in CI — the exact "green local / red CI" gap the scaffold exists to kill, inverted. E2E runs nox and passed, so uv may resolve it — confirm.

=== GAP 3 — .pre-commit-hooks.yaml breaks the single-source claim ===
copier single-sources ruff_version/vulture_version, but this ROOT manifest hardcodes `ruff@0.15.13` (line 5) / `vulture@2.16` (line 12) — not a .jinja, not rendered. Bump the copier var → silent drift. Also arg-less `graph.py --assert` (line 19) defaults to a package name that may not exist in a real project.
It is a stub ("UNUSED in the no-git MVP") — but it IS the remote-delivery path, so it bites the moment the network hop flips. Either render it from the single source, or add a test that asserts its versions == copier vars.

=== GAP 4 — remote/network hop unproven (biggest untested claim) ===
`repo: local → repo:<url> rev:vN` + `copier update` from a real REMOTE — all green locally/WSL, delivery stubbed (see GAP 3). Until a first push + a cross-repo update runs, "versioned central gates" is asserted, not shown. Not a code fix — a verification milestone.

=== MINOR ===
- gitignored sdlc_test/ scratch is a stale pre-refactor instance (still has sdlc_test_viz) — misleading if opened.
- {{project}}_DATA env (ci.yml:67) scaffolds a data-skip pattern the shipped example never exercises — aspirational, harmless.

(Deliberately NOT included: "not yet applied to a real repo" — normal/expected for this stage per the maintainer.)
notes: External read-only audit from the interviews-project Claude session. Provenance: outside agent, not the scaffold maintainer. Cross-check before acting.

title: refresh docs/SPEC.md to post-refactor state (packages list, ship_example, no viewer/import-linter)
description: SPEC.md is pre-refactor: toggle table still lists package_name/has_viewer/viewer_name/viewer_imports_trainer; layer model core<trainer<viewer; import-linter as shipped gate family 5. Post-refactor truth: packages (comma-list) + computed package_name + ship_example; no imposed layering; import-linter is opt-in not shipped; graph.py is the layer-agnostic arch gate; ruff_select + tool versions single-sourced (when:false). Update the toggle table, drop the layer-model section (replace with packages-list + opt-in contracts note), fix the gate-family table (remove import-linter row or mark opt-in), align with the new README. Root README points here.

title: ship_example toggle: make the demo package (math_ops/pipeline) optional for repo adoption
description: The example package math_ops<-pipeline + its unit tests exist so a fresh generation has code to lint/test/graph. When an EXISTING repo adopts the scaffold (copier copy with package_name=their package), the stub collides with / is noise beside their real code and must be manually deleted. Add a ship_example bool toggle (default true). When false: exclude {{ package_name }}/math_ops.py, pipeline.py, tests/unit/test_math_ops.py, test_pipeline.py via _exclude, so adoption yields guardrails-only. Document both paths (new vs adopt) in README. Keep coverage floor sane when example absent (nothing to cover -> floor still passes on empty or the repo's own tests).

title: AUDIT (ext/mindscape): template graph.py missing cardioseg's test-mirror sub-rule
description:
External audit by the mindscape agent while checking devtools sync (mindscape will consume this scaffold).

FINDING: template/devtools/graph.py assert_fitness blocks on god-module + cycle + god-file only. It does NOT carry cardioseg's test-mirror rule — cardioseg/devtools/graph.py defines unmirrored() and does 'blocking += [test mirror ...] for m in unmirrored(packages)' (its graph.py line ~146, GRADUATED to blocking bd j4m2). LEARNINGS.md says the scaffold 'faithfully mirrors cardioseg (most-mature)', but this sub-rule was dropped. So a generated project (and mindscape on consume) loses a gate cardioseg enforces.

IMPACT: mindscape just adopted this rule (its bd 888) as a STRICT path-mirror. Consuming the scaffold as-is would regress it. Both mature siblings (cardioseg + mindscape) enforce test-mirror; the scaffold is the outlier.

SUGGESTED FIX (if accepted): port unmirrored() into template/devtools/graph.py behind enforce_arch_fitness. Use mindscape's more-evolved variant (the superset): strict path-mirror — a logic module <pkg>/<path>/foo.py is mirrored iff tests/unit/<path>/test_foo.py EXISTS — PLUS a coverage-omit shell exemption (unmirrored() reads [tool.coverage] omit; runners/adapters/GPU/download/viz glue exempt so non-unit-testable shells don't force stub tests). Cardioseg exempts only __init__/__main__ (few shells); mindscape's omit carve generalizes better for projects with many shells. Render the [tool.coverage] omit into the generated pyproject via copier. Reference impls: cardiac-seg/devtools/graph.py (strict, minimal) and mindscape/devtools/graph.py (strict + omit exemption).

NOTE: also confirmed the reverse direction is fine — mindscape LACKS the 3 class-shape smell explorers (lcom/data_clumps/state_candidates) the scaffold ships; that is the intended superset mindscape will adopt on consume, not a scaffold defect.

title: refactor: align test-dir naming (end_to_end vs e2e)
description: Scaffold's own suite is tests/end_to_end/; template ships tests/e2e/. Pick one convention across scaffold + template.

title: refactor: declutter scaffold root into docs/
description: Move scaffold's own docs (SPEC.md, LEARNINGS.md) into a docs/ dir; keep README.md + copier.yml at root. Reduces root noise (currently ~11 root files).

title: refactor: single-source pinned tool versions
description: ruff@0.15.13 / vulture@2.16 / nox / pre-commit versions are duplicated across ci.yml, noxfile, pre-commit, e2e.yml, conftest. Single-source via copier.yml computed vars (when:false, like ruff_select) so a bump is one edit.

title: refactor: clean conditional filenames -> copier _exclude
description: Replace all 7 '{% if toggle %}name{% endif %}' conditional filenames/dirs with CLEAN names + jinja _exclude rules in copier.yml (copier renders _exclude with answers). Targets: viewer dir + tests/viewer, devtools sgconfig/sg-rules/jscpd, class-shape trio (lcom/data_clumps/state_candidates -> real .py names). BONUS: real .py extensions let ruff format the trio directly, killing the copy-back hack. VERIFY copier _exclude supports jinja first. Keep {{ package_name }}/{{ viewer_name }} as clean templated names.

title: enable_beads copier toggle (future)
description: Generated projects get bd init + the CLAUDE/AGENTS beads section. All 3 real repos use beads; scaffold does not ship it. Not in the current testing goal.

title: scaffold CI runs tests/unit too
description: e2e.yml: uv run pytest covers tests/unit + tests/end_to_end. Full pytest green in CI.

title: devtools/README.md shipped in template
description: template/devtools/README.md.jinja, toggle-aware: one line per shipped tool (graph/lcom/data_clumps/state_candidates + astgrep/jscpd when on): what it detects + how to run.

title: graph.py --assert inject-test in E2E
description: Parallel to astgrep/jscpd inject tests: write a god-module/cycle into a generated project -> assert graph --assert exits non-zero (gate bites) -> revert -> 0.

title: unit-test graph.py --assert — arch fitness logic
description: Synthetic god-module (fan-in AND fan-out > bottleneck_degree) / import-cycle / file>file_max each fail --assert; clean -> exit 0; [tool.structure] read + defaults merged when absent.

title: unit-test state_candidates.py — shared-param logic
description: >=2 staticmethods sharing a param (>=half), no __init__ -> flagged with param; __init__/pydantic/add_args+run -> skipped. Positive+negative.

title: unit-test data_clumps.py — maximal-set logic
description: {a,b,c} carried whole by >=4 funcs -> that maximal clump reported; support<4 / scattered -> none. Positive+negative.

title: unit-test lcom.py — LCOM4 correctness
description: Feed AST fixtures: 2 disjoint self-field method groups -> lcom4()==2 + components; cohesive class -> 1; interface-impl/abstract/all-trivial -> skipped. Positive+negative.

title: Instability / main-sequence coupling gate in graph.py (I = Ce/(Ce+Ca), distance D)
description:
Near-future. Sharpen the R3 coupling axis with Robert Martin`s package metrics — graph.py already computes the raw afferent/efferent counts, so this is additive.

WHAT graph.py HAS: Ca (afferent = fan-in), Ce (efferent = fan-out), betweenness, on the grimp import graph. Enforces direction (import-linter), acyclicity (no cycles), degree (god-module = Ca AND Ce both high).

WHAT TO ADD:
- Instability I = Ce / (Ce + Ca) per module/package (0 = maximally stable/depended-on, 1 = maximally unstable/depends-on-all). Trivial from the counts already gathered.
- (optional, needs an abstractness signal) Abstractness A = abstract-types / total-types; main-sequence distance D = |A + I - 1| — how far a package sits from the ideal stable<->abstract line (the "V"/stable-dependencies principle: arrows point toward stable abstractions).
- Report: a ranked I (and D if A is computed) table in the graph.py EXPLORER output (drop --assert), beside the existing fan-in/out/bottleneck/chokepoint tables.
- Gate: ADVISORY first (a number to watch), ratchet-to-blocking candidate — e.g. "a module moved further from the main sequence than its recorded baseline" (a per-module D ceiling in [tool.structure], same ratchet shape as file_max / magic-literals). Decide the exact assertion with owner before blocking.

WHY IT`S ITS OWN GATE, not god-module: god-module is a DEGREE check (both counts high). Instability is a RATIO/direction check — it catches a module that is unstable (high Ce) yet widely depended-on (high Ca would lower I, so specifically: depended-on things that are themselves volatile), the stable-dependencies violation a degree threshold misses. Complements, doesn`t duplicate.

OPEN: (a) abstractness signal for A/D — Python has no formal abstract/concrete split; approximate via ABC/Protocol/typing-only modules? or ship I only and defer A/D. (b) module vs package granularity. (c) advisory-forever vs D-ratchet; baseline capture. (d) does this belong in the scaffold superset for all repos or ml-only (it`s domain-neutral -> all repos).

Grounding: README "Coupling, in the standard vocabulary" section; Martin, Agile Software Development (SDP/SAP, the main sequence). Origin: owner insight while rewriting the README pyramid (R3 coupling axis). Relates to the convergence epic vip (a new house-bar gate -> rolls out through the pipe once landed).

title: Tier 3: reconcile the divergent ruff ratchet across the 3 repos (ambiguous placement)
description:
Parent: sdlc-scaffold-vip. Tier 3 = the gap that is REAL but has NO 3-repo consensus yet on whether/how it becomes house-bar. Filed so its tracked, NOT dropped and NOT declared permanent-local (per owner: nothing is permanently project-local; everything should flow through the pipe eventually — this is the "not yet decided where" bucket).

THE DIVERGENCE (audited 2026-07-14): the 3 repos ruff select ratcheted in DIFFERENT directions off the scaffolds narrow common floor:
- synthscape: scaffolds EXACT narrow list (no ratchet).
- mindscape: broad FAMILIES — C4, UP, T20, PLR, PLC, RUF, SIM, C90 (advisory target; enforced subset grows). Deliberately REJECTED N (383 hits).
- cardioseg: ~20 SPECIFIC codes — N (+ a [tool.ruff.lint.pep8-naming] ignore-names block), SLF001, PERF401, C408/C420, S603/S607, PTH123, E741/E742/E743, PLR0124, PLR1714, PLW3301, ICN001, E402, RUF005/007/010/012/022/046, PLW0108, E731.

WHY AMBIGUOUS: these are mostly 1/3 (cardioseg-only) or conflicting (mindscape broad-family vs cardioseg specific-code; N is ON in cardioseg, REJECTED in mindscape). By the scaffolds superset rule (majority of 3) NONE clears the bar today except SIM (already promoted in Tier 1). Promoting cardioseg`s taste wholesale = fitting the house bar to one repo — the exact anti-pattern the rule forbids.

THE WORK (a decision pass, not code-first):
1. Per-code triage across the 3: for each divergent code, is it (a) a bug-finder all 3 should run (-> promote to superset, roll out), (b) opinionated style that conflicts (-> pick ONE house answer WITH owner, or leave out), or (c) genuinely project-specific (-> a LOCAL-SLOT mechanism in the template, identical mechanism / different contents — e.g. the pep8-naming ignore-names is per-repo VOCAB but the block itself can be a shared slot).
2. Resolve the broad-family (mindscape) vs specific-code (cardioseg) STYLE clash — the scaffold must pick one expression of the select so generated config is identical. This is the core "generic architecture identical" tension.
3. Candidates likely to graduate as adoption widens (revisit each as a 2nd/3rd repo picks it up): SLF001, PERF401, the RUF safe-fix set, C408/C420. N + pep8-naming: decide the house line (cardioseg yes / mindscape no) — probably a domain=ml ML-idiom slot rather than a blanket N.
4. Output: an updated scaffold ruff_select (single source) + a pep8-naming LOCAL-SLOT mechanism, then the copier-update rollout so all 3 reconverge onto the SAME select (their only difference = LOCAL-SLOT vocab).

This is intentionally the slow tier — start Tier 1+2, widen adoption, come back and let evidence (2nd/3rd-repo uptake) decide each code. Origin: cardiac-seg ruff bd l79o / l5q8; mindscape + synthscape pyproject audits.

title: cleanup: unwrap now-inert {% if enable_X %} conditionals + SPEC gate-inventory polish
description:
@
After rji (gates removed -> always-on) + r2w (zero code), the enable_astgrep/jscpd/class_shape/beads/enforce_arch_fitness are computed when:false=true, so their {% raw %}{% if X %}{% endraw %} blocks in ci/nox/pre-commit/pyproject/_partials are dead-always-true. Cosmetic: unwrap them (remove the conditional, keep the body) so a reader does not wonder when they are false. Also SPEC gate-inventory rows still label gates with stale (enable_X) toggle names + the example-code section describes a shipped demo that no longer ships. Low priority; behavior is correct + tested (59 passed). use_import_linter conditional STAYS (real: >1 package).
close_reason: Unwrapped all inert {% if enable_X %} guards across nox/ci/pre-commit/pyproject/gitignore/_partials/READMEs; dropped 5 dead computed flags from copier.yml; SPEC polished. 59 passed.

title: Soft data-convention assumption baked in (paths.yaml / {project}_DATA) — trim or toggle
description: From 8fw audit (other structural assumptions sweep). The scaffold assumes a data-outside-repo workflow it does NOT gate: template/.gitignore ignores paths.yaml + data/; template ci.yml sets {{project}}_DATA=/tmp/nodata (a data-skip env for data-gated tests). Neither is enforced and the shipped example never uses them (aspirational, also flagged in hn8). Harmless if unused (ignores an absent file; sets an env nothing reads) but an unearned assumption about the project data layout. Prefer TRIMMING the inert bits (drop the _DATA ci env since nothing reads it; the paths.yaml gitignore line is a harmless default, keep or drop) over adding a toggle. Owner: keep toggles minimal.
close_reason: resolved: paths.yaml + {project}_DATA now gated under enable_ml (v0.5.0)

title: remote: scaffold-side smoke test/CI for the remote copier-update path
description: Once the remote flow works manually, add a scripted smoke (or a scaffold CI job) that exercises copier update from the remote URL end-to-end, so the network path is a regression-guarded gate like the local E2E — not a one-time manual check. Closes the 'asserted not shown' gap in sdlc-scaffold-8r7 permanently.
close_reason: Remote copier-update network smoke: tests/e2e/test_remote_update.py (copy@tag -> update@newer-tag over real remote URL, asserts _commit bump) + .github/workflows/remote-smoke.yml (weekly+dispatch). Secure extraheader auth (no token in argv). Live proof PASSED 5.59s; default-skips offline.
